summary: The current init output is technically coherent but hard to read for humans: bootstrap, taxonomy, rules, evidence, and metadata share the .fabric surface without an obvious information architecture. The safest direction is to preserve .fabric/rules as the source tree, improve layer visibility, treat taxonomy as a draft, and keep bootstrap as a protocol entrypoint unless a planned migration changes compatibility points.

key_conclusions.point: Do not directly migrate to rules/l0|l1|l2 without a compatibility plan.
key_conclusions.evidence: Layer derivation currently depends on path depth and _cross segments.

key_conclusions.point: Bootstrap README should remain separate from rule bodies for now.
key_conclusions.evidence: It is referenced as .fabric/bootstrap/README.md by init, doctor, hooks, bootstrap-guide, and meta builder.

key_conclusions.point: INITIAL_TAXONOMY should be reframed as a generated draft and likely moved under a taxonomy folder.
key_conclusions.evidence: It is generated by buildInitialTaxonomyMarkdown from forensicReport.

key_conclusions.point: Chinese MUST/NEVER rule bodies are viable under stable English section headings.
key_conclusions.evidence: parseRuleSections extracts Chinese text under structured sections in tests.

recommendations.action: Improve layer visibility without immediately changing rule source paths.
recommendations.rationale: This solves the user's readability concern while avoiding a breaking migration.

steps.description: Add explicit layer summaries to taxonomy or doctor output.
steps.verification: init output shows L0/L1/L2 mapping clearly.

recommendations.action: Keep bootstrap as .fabric/bootstrap/README.md and optionally add a structured summary later.
recommendations.rationale: Bootstrap is a protocol contract used by multiple components.

steps.description: Document bootstrap as protocol entrypoint, not rule body.
steps.verification: Generated bootstrap clearly explains the distinction.

recommendations.action: Move generated taxonomy into .fabric/taxonomy with backward compatibility.
recommendations.rationale: Taxonomy is planning metadata, not a runtime rule source.

steps.description: Write new taxonomy path and keep old path accepted during migration.
steps.verification: doctor accepts both paths during compatibility window.

open_questions: Whether to make Chinese rule body templates the default for zh-CN users.
Whether bootstrap should ever become a structured rule file or remain a protocol-only document.

intent_coverage.notes: Generated by CLI from forensic report.
Supported when section headings and protected tokens remain stable.
Confirmed and attributed to protocol-entrypoint role.
Context and evidence, not hard constraints.

findings_coverage.finding: Layer is metadata but not human-visible in tree.
Bootstrap is special L0 protocol entrypoint.
Taxonomy is generated from forensic report.
